title: StrategyExample documents different thresholds than it implements

evidence.quote: a position should be opened if the price moves above 70, and the position should be closed if a position moves below 30.
NewCrossUpIndicatorRule(entryConstant, indicator) ... moves above 30 ... NewCrossDownIndicatorRule(indicator, exitConstant) ... moves below 10

reasoning: The function-level documentation promises 70/30 thresholds, while the actual rules and inline comments use 30/10. Readers cannot tell which strategy is intended, and copy-pasted code will not match the documented behavior.
recommendation: Align the function comment, constants, and inline comments on one set of thresholds.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No linked tests assert the documented StrategyExample thresholds or exercise the example strategy behavior.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add an example test that constructs the strategy with named threshold constants and verifies the documented entry and exit thresholds are the ones used by the rules.
minimumFixScope: Update example/strategy.go comments or constants so the public example is internally consistent.
